chunks.text: Sylvia Ratnasamy is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Berkeley, whose work focuses on the design and implementation of networked systems. She received her PhD in Computer Science from U.C.Berkeley in 2002 and a Bachelors in Computer Engineering from the University of Pune, India, in 1997. She is a recipient of the ACM Grace Murray Hopper award, the ACM SIGCOMM Test-of-Time award, the ACM SIGCOMM Rising Star award and the Alfred P. Sloan research fellowship.

Research Areas: Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT). Research Centers: International Computer Science Institute (ICSI), Networked Systems Lab (NETSYS), Software Principles for Advanced Networking (SPAN).

Selected Publications: E. Amaro et al., 'Can far memory improve job throughput?,' in Proceedings of the Fifteenth European Conference on Computer Systems, 2020, pp. 1-16. J. Sherry et al., 'Making middleboxes someone else's problem: network processing as a cloud service,' in Proceedings of the ACM SIGCOMM 2012 conference, SIGCOMM '12, New York, NY, USA: ACM, 2012, pp. 13-24.

Selected Honors and Awards: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Fellow, 2025; N² Women: Star in Computer Networking and Communications, 2019; N² Women: Rising Stars in Computer Networking and Communications, 2016; Grace Murray Hopper Award, 2014; Okawa Research Grant, 2013; Sloan Research Fellow, 2012; ACM SIGCOMM Test of Time Paper Award, 2011.
